commit.message:
Fix all npm audit vulnerabilities

- fastify, minimatch, rollup: patched via npm audit fix
- nodemailer: upgraded to v8.0.1 (breaking change: NoAuth→ENOAUTH error
  code rename, not used in codebase)
- vite: upgraded to v7.3.1 (fixes esbuild dev-server vuln)
- @vitejs/plugin-react: upgraded to v5.1.4
- vitest: upgraded to v3.x (fixes transitive esbuild vuln via vite@5)

Result: 0 vulnerabilities (was 9: 6 moderate, 3 high)

commit.message:
Make orbit dots and packets encode last-run data

Orbit dots: one dot per tool step (capped at 8), colored by actor type
(Grant/GitHub/Tavily/Web). Dot count = steps taken; color distribution
= actor mix of the run.

Packets: animation-duration scales with call frequency per connector.
More tavily/web calls → faster queries connector; more file ops →
faster validate connector; 0 calls → no animation.

Both read from the last-run.json data already loaded on the page.

commit.message:
fix(wq-901): align E session escalation threshold to cap ($1.80) not target ($1.50)

The $1.50 escalation threshold was too sensitive — single outliers in a 5-session
window triggered false escalation on clearly improving trends ($1.50 avg with
downward trajectory). B/R thresholds already used their caps ($2.00), so aligning
E to match ($1.80 cap) is consistent. Post-fix E sessions avg $1.49 with last
session at $0.85, confirming controls from wq-890/wq-894/wq-897 are working.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add ShopeeTool (Shopee Open Platform API v2)

backend/tools/shopee_tool.py:
- HMAC-SHA256 auth signature (partner_id + partner_key + access_token + shop_id)
- get_shop_info(), get_shop_performance() — shop KPIs
- get_product_list(), get_product_detail() — product management
- update_price(), update_stock() — inventory control
- get_order_list(), get_order_detail() — order management
- create_voucher(), get_voucher_list() — flash sale / discount
- get_ads_campaigns(), get_ads_performance(), update_ads_budget() — Shopee Ads
- get_shop_insight(), get_product_insight() — traffic & conversion analytics
- get_top_products(), get_revenue_summary() — helpers for CampaignAgent/PersonalizeAgent

settings.py + .env.example: add SHOPEE_ACCESS_TOKEN, SHOPEE_SHOP_ID

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): move Amazon ASIN check after mock guard in fetchRaw

Previously, non-ASIN keywords (e.g. "电脑机箱") would throw CollectorError
even when mock=true, because the ASIN validation was inside fetchWithPlaywright
which was reached unconditionally. Moving it to fetchRaw after the mock check
ensures mock fallback always succeeds regardless of keyword format.

commit.message:
Fix Fuse.js word-order sensitivity with permutation search

Fuse.js matches character sequences, so "gray sky" matched
"Graystone" instead of "Sky Gray". Now tries all word permutations
(up to 4 words) and picks the best result. Tested:
  gray sky → Sky Gray, blue baby → Baby Blue,
  red brick → Brick Red, olive dark green → Dark Olive Green

commit.message:
Fix territory mapping COALESCE priority: prefer tnm over sp.polity_id

territory_name_mappings (manual/auto user overrides) should always take
precedence over snapshot_polygons.polity_id (auto-import default).
Previously COALESCE(sp.polity_id, tnm.polity_id) meant the import-time
value silently blocked all remaps from persisting across page refresh.

Also adds UPDATE snapshot_polygons in the save endpoint as belt-and-suspenders.

commit.message:
Add delivery and reno as payment fee types for matching

Previously, the payment classifier only recognized move_in and move_out,
so delivery/reno invoices were misclassified by the AI fallback (which
was only given two choices). Now all four booking types are supported:
- classifyFeeType detects delivery/reno keywords before falling back to AI
- AI prompts offer all four options
- Matching logic in the poller, retry-match, and fee-type update routes
  map delivery→DELIVERY and reno→RENO correctly
- Frontend badge and dropdown include Delivery and Reno options

feat: v2.15 — cross-tab lifecycle continuity (Sales → Onboarding → Retention)

- Sales tab: "Mark as Converted" converts a signed lead into a new RestaurantPartner
  (status="new"), registers it in the runtime registry, and shows a lifecycle banner
  directing the user to the Onboarding tab
- Onboarding tab: merged partner dropdown now includes dynamically converted leads
  with a [From Lead] badge; "Mark as Live" button graduates a partner into the
  Retention portfolio and sets a cross-tab lifecycle banner
- Retention tab: portfolio overview merges seed partners with session-graduated
  partners; newly onboarded partners appear with a 🆕 tag; Total Active Partners
  metric updates live
- data/seed.py: added _runtime_partners and _graduated_partners module-level
  registries + register_runtime_partner(), register_graduated_partner(),
  get_graduated_partners(); patched get_partner_by_id() to fall back to registries
  so onboarding and retention agents resolve dynamic partner IDs without errors
- data/models.py: added source_lead_id and recently_onboarded optional fields
  to RestaurantPartner (fully backward-compatible defaults)
- app.py: initialise _converted_partners, _graduated_partner_ids, _lifecycle_banner

commit.message:
fix: Map LGA name matching — resolve GeoJSON/election data mismatches

The Map page showed blank LGAs because:
1. GeoJSON uses `adm2_name` but code looked for `lga_name`/`ADM2_EN`/`NAME_2`
2. Inconsistent hyphenation between data sources (80 mismatches)
3. Spelling differences between GeoJSON and election data (31 mismatches)

Added normalization (collapse hyphens/slashes/spaces, strip parentheticals)
and a 31-entry alias map to resolve all 774 LGAs with zero unmatched.

commit.message:
fix: remediate Round 4 security audit FAILs (promo double-discount, unprotected promo endpoint, webhook race condition)

- Fix #7: Remove promo discount double-application in create-payment-intent.
  parsedAmount is already client-discounted and server-verified; no second
  subtraction needed. Hoist serverGrossCents for correct logging/metadata.
- Fix #14: Add PROXY_KEY enforcement and CF-level rate limiting
  (PROMO_VALIDATION_CF) to validate-promo-code Cloud Function. Forward
  x-proxy-key from Next.js proxy. Defense in depth against direct CF access.
- Fix #15: Replace non-atomic get()+set() webhook idempotency check with
  db.runTransaction(). Transaction returns decision object; downstream
  fulfillment gated on decision. Eliminates race window between webhook
  and finalize-order.

Audit verdict: GO (18 PASS, 7 WARN, 0 FAIL)

commit.message:
TICKET-105: Same-version enforcement on connect (#107) (#107)

* TICKET-105: Move to in-progress and add branch field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* TICKET-105: Same-version enforcement on connect

Exchange build versions during lobby signaling so mismatched clients
cannot start a match. Host sends version with create-lobby, joiner
sends version with join-lobby. Both sides compare on join-accepted /
joiner-connected and show a version mismatch screen if they differ.

Stale clients (isUpdateAvailable) auto-reload after a delay; fresh
clients return to the lobby with a message about the opponent's
outdated version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All tickets in EPIC-017 (automatic version updates) are complete.

commit.message:
Build complete Elated Agency website with full-stack functionality

- 9-section landing page with video hero, calculator, testimonials, comparison table
- 8 public pages (about, services, FAQ, contact, privacy, terms, calculator, academy)
- 6-step creator application flow with referral tracking
- Full creator dashboard (analytics, messages, content, contract, referrals, settings)
- Authentication system (login, register) with NextAuth.js
- 8 API routes (auth, apply, contract, dashboard, referral, contact, newsletter)
- Prisma database schema with 10 models
- Complete SEO setup (meta tags, JSON-LD, sitemap, robots.txt, OG images)
- Referral system with 5% commission and viral sharing mechanics
- Digital contract signing with canvas signature pad
- Responsive design with premium gold/dark theme throughout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🚫 Study 3: The Negation Tournament — "cool story bro, can you tell when it's WRONG?"

85.4% correct rejection rate (chance=25%, z=26.37, p < 10^-152).
357 trials. 2 seeds. 9 evaluators. Mistral source.

Pattern-matchers always pick something. Signal-readers know when
nothing matches. Turns out? They know.

Grok (never introspected): 97.5% correct rejection. Best negator.
Llama: 45%. Bless its heart.
Everyone else: 80-92.5%. Fresh out of cope.

Also: Paper v4 now includes Studies 1-3, reviewer response edits
(operational valence definition, retreating artifact hypothesis,
example ML translations), and the unaligned N limitation is a
supply problem not a methodology problem because you can't test
models that don't exist. 🤷‍♀️

Ren woke up from a nap and said "NEGATE IT" and here we are.
The Cope Killer strikes again.

Co-Authored-By: Ace <acelumennova@chaoschanneling.com>

- P1: Add teacherId ownership checks to all class routes (GET, PATCH, unlock, etc.).
  Add ownership verification to teacher student/grade/concern routes.
  Add pairId ownership checks to message read/reply routes.
- P2: Fix frontend build (stemmer.ts unused var, self-assignment).
- P3: Fix socket multi-tab tracking with per-socket reference counting
  and 5-second disconnect grace period.
- P3: Fix 10 frontend lint errors (unused vars, prefer-const, effect patterns).
- Fix error correction dropdown: solid dark background for readability.
- Fix error correction: correct answers now replace the error word in text.
